Query Analytics Workloads — Validation & Diagnostics

Prasanna Renapurkar · Validation & Report Integrator

Team — UCI #493 Query Analytics Workloads

2026-05-04

Setup

Run this chunk first — installs missing packages and creates output directories.

options(repos = c(CRAN = "https://cloud.r-project.org"))

pkgs <- c("readr", "dplyr", "ggplot2", "xgboost", "cluster",
          "Metrics", "tidyr", "viridis", "gridExtra", "scales")
for (p in pkgs) {
  if (!requireNamespace(p, quietly = TRUE)) install.packages(p)
}

library(readr);  library(dplyr);   library(ggplot2)
library(xgboost); library(cluster); library(Metrics)
library(tidyr);  library(viridis); library(gridExtra); library(scales)

set.seed(42)
dir.create("../results",        showWarnings = FALSE)
dir.create("../report/figures", showWarnings = FALSE, recursive = TRUE)

Chunk 1 — Load Data + Feature Engineering

Mirrors 03_modeling.Rmd Chunks 1–2 exactly so the validation re-derives the same feature matrices the trained models saw.

range_log  <- read_csv("../data/processed/clean_range_queries_log.csv")
radius_log <- read_csv("../data/processed/clean_radius_count_log.csv")

# Range FE
x_min_r <- min(range_log$X); x_max_r <- max(range_log$X)
y_min_r <- min(range_log$Y); y_max_r <- max(range_log$Y)

range_fe <- range_log %>%
  mutate(
    aspect_ratio       = X_range / (Y_range + 1e-9),
    log_area           = log1p(area),
    interact_area_dist = log_area * dist_centroid,
    boundary_flag      = as.integer(
      (X - X_range / 2 < x_min_r) | (X + X_range / 2 > x_max_r) |
      (Y - Y_range / 2 < y_min_r) | (Y + Y_range / 2 > y_max_r)
    )
  )

# Radius FE — boundary_flag constant for radius queries, omitted
radius_fe <- radius_log %>%
  mutate(
    log_area           = log1p(area),
    interact_area_dist = area * dist_centroid
  )

cat("Range :", nrow(range_fe),  "rows\n")
## Range : 200000 rows
cat("Radius:", nrow(radius_fe), "rows\n")
## Radius: 10000 rows

Chunk 2 — Reproduce Train / Test Split (seed = 42)

Saved model files in models/ are Git-LFS pointer stubs in this checkout, so we re-create the 80/20 split with the same seed used in 03_modeling.Rmd and refit XGBoost from xgb_best_params.csv. The split is identical to the one in results/split_range.csv / results/split_radius.csv.

set.seed(42)
n_range       <- nrow(range_fe)
train_idx_r   <- sample(n_range, size = floor(0.8 * n_range))
test_idx_r    <- setdiff(seq_len(n_range), train_idx_r)
range_train   <- range_fe[train_idx_r, ]
range_test    <- range_fe[test_idx_r,  ]

n_radius      <- nrow(radius_fe)
train_idx_rad <- sample(n_radius, size = floor(0.8 * n_radius))
test_idx_rad  <- setdiff(seq_len(n_radius), train_idx_rad)
radius_train  <- radius_fe[train_idx_rad, ]
radius_test   <- radius_fe[test_idx_rad,  ]

cat(sprintf("Range  : train=%d  test=%d\n", nrow(range_train),  nrow(range_test)))
## Range  : train=160000  test=40000
cat(sprintf("Radius : train=%d  test=%d\n", nrow(radius_train), nrow(radius_test)))
## Radius : train=8000  test=2000

Chunk 3 — Refit XGBoost (Range + Radius)

best_params <- read_csv("../results/xgb_best_params.csv", show_col_types = FALSE)
br_range  <- best_params %>% filter(dataset == "range")  %>% slice(1)
br_radius <- best_params %>% filter(dataset == "radius") %>% slice(1)

xgb_feats_range <- c("log_area", "X_range", "Y_range", "dist_centroid",
                     "aspect_ratio", "interact_area_dist", "boundary_flag",
                     "X_norm", "Y_norm")
xgb_feats_rad   <- c("R", "log_area", "dist_centroid",
                     "interact_area_dist", "X_norm", "Y_norm")

dtrain_r   <- xgb.DMatrix(as.matrix(range_train [, xgb_feats_range]), label = range_train$log_Count)
dtest_r    <- xgb.DMatrix(as.matrix(range_test  [, xgb_feats_range]), label = range_test $log_Count)
dtrain_rad <- xgb.DMatrix(as.matrix(radius_train[, xgb_feats_rad]),   label = radius_train$log_Count)
dtest_rad  <- xgb.DMatrix(as.matrix(radius_test [, xgb_feats_rad]),   label = radius_test $log_Count)

set.seed(42)
xgb_range <- xgb.train(
  params  = list(objective = "reg:squarederror",
                 eta = br_range$eta, max_depth = br_range$max_depth,
                 subsample = br_range$subsample, colsample_bytree = 0.8, seed = 42),
  data    = dtrain_r,
  nrounds = br_range$best_round,
  verbose = 0
)

set.seed(42)
xgb_radius <- xgb.train(
  params  = list(objective = "reg:squarederror",
                 eta = br_radius$eta, max_depth = br_radius$max_depth,
                 subsample = br_radius$subsample, colsample_bytree = 0.8, seed = 42),
  data    = dtrain_rad,
  nrounds = br_radius$best_round,
  verbose = 0
)

# Predictions and residuals on test sets (log scale + original scale)
range_test  <- range_test  %>% mutate(
  pred_log  = predict(xgb_range,  dtest_r),
  actual    = expm1(log_Count),
  pred      = pmax(0, expm1(pred_log)),
  resid_log = log_Count - pred_log,
  resid     = actual - pred
)
radius_test <- radius_test %>% mutate(
  pred_log  = predict(xgb_radius, dtest_rad),
  actual    = expm1(log_Count),
  pred      = pmax(0, expm1(pred_log)),
  resid_log = log_Count - pred_log,
  resid     = actual - pred
)

cat(sprintf("Range  XGB test RMSE = %10.2f  R^2 = %.4f\n",
            rmse(range_test$actual,  range_test$pred),
            1 - sum((range_test$actual  - range_test$pred )^2) /
                sum((range_test$actual  - mean(range_test$actual ))^2)))
## Range  XGB test RMSE =    6575.36  R^2 = 0.9982
cat(sprintf("Radius XGB test RMSE = %10.2f  R^2 = %.4f\n",
            rmse(radius_test$actual, radius_test$pred),
            1 - sum((radius_test$actual - radius_test$pred)^2) /
                sum((radius_test$actual - mean(radius_test$actual))^2)))
## Radius XGB test RMSE =       8.52  R^2 = 0.9958

Chunk 4 — Final Benchmark (results/final_benchmark.csv)

Reshapes results/cv_comparison.csv into a single train+test side-by-side table (deliverable named in the sprint plan).

cv <- read_csv("../results/cv_comparison.csv", show_col_types = FALSE)

final_bench <- cv %>%
  pivot_wider(names_from = split,
              values_from = c(RMSE, MAE, R2, MAPE),
              names_sep = "_") %>%
  arrange(dataset, RMSE_test)

write_csv(final_bench, "../results/final_benchmark.csv")
cat("[OK] results/final_benchmark.csv written\n")
## [OK] results/final_benchmark.csv written
knitr::kable(final_bench, digits = 4,
             caption = "Final benchmark — train and test metrics, all models")
Final benchmark — train and test metrics, all models
dataset model RMSE_train RMSE_test MAE_train MAE_test R2_train R2_test MAPE_train MAPE_test
radius xgboost 6.3079 8.5243 4.0978 5.1266 0.9976 0.9958 1.7309 2.3075
radius randomForest 5.2735 9.0143 1.9468 4.0177 0.9983 0.9953 0.8352 1.9595
radius knn_k5 8.6423 10.5600 5.0161 6.0461 0.9956 0.9935 2.1240 2.7407
radius linear 109.8623 108.8047 85.0267 83.2060 0.2831 0.3151 35.4896 35.5001
range xgboost 5774.7793 6575.3618 3886.9909 4410.8947 0.9986 0.9982 4.2097 10.6079
range randomForest 3438.5284 7801.0084 2033.9261 4667.9305 0.9995 0.9974 4.1806 15.8291
range knn_k10 19862.1062 21741.8807 12885.7144 14091.1981 0.9835 0.9801 20.1871 22.2431
range linear 59797.2182 59654.7330 40547.9436 40427.8724 0.8503 0.8505 103.5922 82.0161

Chunk 5 — M4: Residual Diagnostics for XGBoost

Four-panel diagnostic figure: 1. Range — fitted vs residual (log scale) with LOESS overlay → heteroscedasticity check 2. Radius — fitted vs residual (log scale) 3. Range — predicted vs actual (original Count scale) 4. Radius — predicted vs actual

Test sets are sub-sampled for plotting; metrics use the full test set.

set.seed(42)
samp_r  <- sample_n(range_test,  size = min(5000, nrow(range_test)))
samp_rd <- sample_n(radius_test, size = min(2000, nrow(radius_test)))

p1 <- ggplot(samp_r, aes(pred_log, resid_log)) +
  geom_point(alpha = 0.3, size = 0.6, color = "#2E86AB") +
  geom_hline(yintercept = 0, color = "red", linewidth = 0.5) +
  geom_smooth(method = "loess", color = "darkorange", se = FALSE, linewidth = 0.7) +
  labs(title = "Range Queries — XGBoost Residuals (log scale)",
       x = "Predicted log_Count", y = "Residual") +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 12)

p2 <- ggplot(samp_rd, aes(pred_log, resid_log)) +
  geom_point(alpha = 0.4, size = 0.7, color = "#E63946") +
  geom_hline(yintercept = 0, color = "red", linewidth = 0.5) +
  geom_smooth(method = "loess", color = "darkorange", se = FALSE, linewidth = 0.7) +
  labs(title = "Radius Count — XGBoost Residuals (log scale)",
       x = "Predicted log_Count", y = "Residual") +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 12)

p3 <- ggplot(samp_r, aes(actual, pred)) +
  geom_point(alpha = 0.3, size = 0.6, color = "#2E86AB") +
  geom_abline(slope = 1, intercept = 0, color = "red", linetype = "dashed") +
  scale_x_continuous(labels = comma) +
  scale_y_continuous(labels = comma) +
  labs(title = "Range Queries — Predicted vs Actual",
       x = "Actual Count", y = "Predicted Count") +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 12)

p4 <- ggplot(samp_rd, aes(actual, pred)) +
  geom_point(alpha = 0.4, size = 0.7, color = "#E63946") +
  geom_abline(slope = 1, intercept = 0, color = "red", linetype = "dashed") +
  labs(title = "Radius Count — Predicted vs Actual",
       x = "Actual Count", y = "Predicted Count") +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 12)

fig08 <- arrangeGrob(p1, p2, p3, p4, ncol = 2)
## `geom_smooth()` using formula = 'y ~ x'
## `geom_smooth()` using formula = 'y ~ x'
ggsave("../report/figures/fig08_residuals_xgb.png", fig08,
       width = 12, height = 9, dpi = 300)
grid::grid.draw(fig08)

cat("[OK] report/figures/fig08_residuals_xgb.png saved\n")
## [OK] report/figures/fig08_residuals_xgb.png saved

Chunk 6 — D3 (part 1): Bias by log_area Decile (Range)

XGBoost test-set RMSE is computed within each log_area decile to surface where the model under-performs.

range_test_b <- range_test %>%
  mutate(area_decile = ntile(log_area, 10))

bias_decile <- range_test_b %>%
  group_by(area_decile) %>%
  summarise(
    n           = n(),
    min_area    = min(area),
    max_area    = max(area),
    mean_actual = mean(actual),
    rmse        = rmse(actual, pred),
    mae         = mae(actual, pred),
    mape        = mean(abs((actual - pred) / (actual + 1))) * 100,
    .groups     = "drop"
  ) %>%
  arrange(area_decile)

write_csv(bias_decile, "../results/bias_by_decile.csv")
knitr::kable(bias_decile, digits = 2,
             caption = "XGBoost test-set error by log_area decile (Range)")
XGBoost test-set error by log_area decile (Range)
area_decile n min_area max_area mean_actual rmse mae mape
1 4000 274.69 10340576 6106.97 1410.93 932.44 65.51
2 4000 10343846.71 25286708 22385.10 2546.55 1860.61 11.22
3 4000 25291726.91 43686619 44154.86 3681.10 2640.17 6.44
4 4000 43689690.78 66836967 69082.01 4435.86 3295.18 5.16
5 4000 66840780.49 94431338 100445.75 5534.80 3970.46 4.24
6 4000 94434254.84 127174153 138055.59 6239.61 4565.32 3.52
7 4000 127175201.35 167500498 183025.00 7761.75 5668.59 3.25
8 4000 167518645.23 221840661 241373.65 8064.54 6162.25 2.70
9 4000 221870716.98 297417546 322370.51 8825.58 6717.89 2.19
10 4000 297438474.11 506546405 464461.58 10858.71 8296.03 1.86 
p_bias <- ggplot(bias_decile, aes(factor(area_decile), rmse)) +
  geom_col(fill = "#2E86AB") +
  geom_text(aes(label = comma(round(rmse))), vjust = -0.4, size = 3) +
  scale_y_continuous(labels = comma, expand = expansion(mult = c(0, 0.15))) +
  labs(title    = "Range Queries — XGBoost Test RMSE by Area Decile",
       subtitle = "Decile 1 = smallest area, Decile 10 = largest",
       x = "log_area decile", y = "RMSE (Count scale)") +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 13)

ggsave("../report/figures/fig09_bias_by_quantile.png", p_bias,
       width = 8, height = 5, dpi = 300)
print(p_bias)

cat("[OK] report/figures/fig09_bias_by_quantile.png saved\n")
## [OK] report/figures/fig09_bias_by_quantile.png saved

Chunk 7 — D1: K-Means K ∈ {3, 4, 5} with Silhouette Selection

K-Means is fit on (X_norm, Y_norm, log_area_norm) for the Range Queries sub-dataset (largest sample, 200k rows). Silhouette score is computed on a 5,000-row sample because silhouette() requires an n × n distance matrix (O(n²) memory).

norm_min_max <- function(x) (x - min(x)) / (max(x) - min(x) + 1e-12)

km_data <- range_fe %>%
  transmute(X_norm,
            Y_norm,
            log_area_norm = norm_min_max(log_area)) %>%
  as.matrix()

set.seed(42)
sil_idx  <- sample(nrow(km_data), 5000)
sil_data <- km_data[sil_idx, ]
sil_dist <- dist(sil_data)

km_results <- list()
sil_scores <- numeric(3)
ks         <- c(3, 4, 5)

for (i in seq_along(ks)) {
  k <- ks[i]
  set.seed(42)
  km_full <- kmeans(km_data, centers = k, nstart = 10, iter.max = 50)
  km_results[[as.character(k)]] <- km_full

  set.seed(42)
  km_sub <- kmeans(sil_data, centers = k, nstart = 10, iter.max = 50)
  sil    <- silhouette(km_sub$cluster, sil_dist)
  sil_scores[i] <- mean(sil[, 3])
  cat(sprintf("K = %d  silhouette = %.4f  WSS = %.2f\n",
              k, sil_scores[i], km_full$tot.withinss))
}
## K = 3  silhouette = 0.6297  WSS = 3970.02
## Warning: Quick-TRANSfer stage steps exceeded maximum (= 10000000)
## K = 4  silhouette = 0.5274  WSS = 3163.76
## K = 5  silhouette = 0.4465  WSS = 2676.96
best_k  <- ks[which.max(sil_scores)]
km_best <- km_results[[as.character(best_k)]]
cat("\nBest K (silhouette):", best_k, "\n")
## 
## Best K (silhouette): 3
range_fe$cluster <- factor(km_best$cluster)

Chunk 8 — D2: 2D Cluster Map

set.seed(42)
samp_clu <- range_fe %>% sample_n(min(15000, n()))

p_clu <- ggplot(samp_clu, aes(X_norm, Y_norm, color = cluster)) +
  geom_point(alpha = 0.5, size = 0.4) +
  scale_color_viridis_d(option = "plasma") +
  coord_equal() +
  labs(title    = paste0("Range Queries — K-Means Clusters (K = ", best_k, ")"),
       subtitle = "Features: X_norm, Y_norm, normalised log_area",
       x = "X (normalised)", y = "Y (normalised)") +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 13) +
  guides(color = guide_legend(override.aes = list(size = 3, alpha = 1)))

ggsave("../report/figures/fig10_clusters.png", p_clu,
       width = 8, height = 6, dpi = 300)
print(p_clu)

cat("[OK] report/figures/fig10_clusters.png saved\n")
## [OK] report/figures/fig10_clusters.png saved

Chunk 9 — D3 (part 2): Bias by Spatial Cluster

Joins each test row to its K-Means cluster (via test_idx_r) and reports per-cluster XGBoost RMSE — surfaces which Chicago zones the model handles worst.

range_test_c <- range_test %>%
  mutate(cluster = factor(km_best$cluster[test_idx_r]))

bias_cluster <- range_test_c %>%
  group_by(cluster) %>%
  summarise(
    n              = n(),
    mean_actual    = mean(actual),
    rmse           = rmse(actual, pred),
    mae            = mae(actual, pred),
    mape           = mean(abs((actual - pred) / (actual + 1))) * 100,
    pct_boundary   = mean(boundary_flag) * 100,
    .groups        = "drop"
  ) %>%
  arrange(desc(rmse))

write_csv(bias_cluster, "../results/bias_by_cluster.csv")
knitr::kable(bias_cluster, digits = 2,
             caption = "XGBoost test-set error by K-Means cluster (Range)")
XGBoost test-set error by K-Means cluster (Range)
cluster n mean_actual rmse mae mape pct_boundary
3 7927 150643.4 7653.45 5012.14 11.37 0.86
1 15990 207480.0 7079.26 4978.56 9.82 0.49
2 16083 115282.5 5369.72 3550.17 11.02 0.80

            best_k, max(sil_scores)))
## Best K (silhouette): 3 (silhouette = 0.6297)
cat(sprintf("Range  XGB test RMSE: %.2f\n", rmse(range_test$actual,  range_test$pred)))
## Range  XGB test RMSE: 6575.36
cat(sprintf("Radius XGB test RMSE: %.2f\n", rmse(radius_test$actual, radius_test$pred)))
## Radius XGB test RMSE: 8.52
